narrative: JSW Steel remained a strong ESG all-rounder in FY2025, with 35 rank-one indicators, 24 rank-two indicators and only 10 rank-four outcomes. Its main environmental strength was renewable energy scale: it led both renewable energy and renewable electricity, reaching 3.23 million GJ and 3.06 million GJ respectively. It also remained the best operational water-intensity performer at 2.369 kL/tcs and reported zero discharge, making it the most relevant water-efficiency benchmark for Tata. Product responsibility was another strong area, with 100% coverage for environmental/social product information and recycling or safe-disposal information. Weaknesses were concentrated in air emissions, governance concentration and wellbeing. JSW ranked last on SOx, NOx and particulate matter in FY2025, indicating that renewable power progress had not translated into leadership on local air pollutants. It was also weak on wellbeing expenditure and related-party investment concentration. Socially, JSW sustained 100% training coverage and remained competitive on fatalities, but worker TRIFR and affected-worker outcomes worsened relative to the leading peer. Financially, it stayed close to Tata on turnover and led net worth where Tata’s standardised value was missing. Overall, JSW’s FY2025 profile is that of a high-scale, efficiency-oriented competitor with strong renewable procurement, water performance and product disclosure, but with meaningful gaps in air emissions, welfare intensity and RPT governance exposure.
